description_long: This is a compact, handheld two-way radio transceiver, commonly known as a walkie-talkie. The device features a prominent black antenna at the top, a textured speaker grille on the front, and a combination of black and olive green casing. There are two rotary dials on the top, likely for power/volume and channel selection, and a push-to-talk button on the side. The back of the device is flat with visible screws and a battery compartment cover, while the sides have additional buttons and a belt clip. The overall design is rugged, suitable for outdoor or urban use, and the materials suggest durability and resistance to impact.
description: A rugged, handheld walkie-talkie with a black antenna, olive green and black casing, and multiple control buttons and dials.
description_view_0: Front view showing the speaker grille, antenna, and top control dials.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the belt clip and side push-to-talk button.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the battery compartment cover and mounting screws.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with additional side controls and the belt clip visible.
